events.title: NEET results canceled
events.body: Authorities cancel results and order a re-exam over paper-leak allegations; at least 13 student suicides are reported in surrounding weeks.

events.title: Movement begins online
events.body: Created as political satire, named after a Chief Justice remark comparing unemployed youth to cockroaches; spreads rapidly on social media.

events.title: Jantar Mantar camp
events.body: Online momentum turns into permitted demonstrations at Jantar Mantar, which becomes the movement's headquarters.

events.title: Hunger strike begins
events.body: Activist Sonam Wangchuk joins and starts an indefinite hunger strike demanding the education minister's resignation.

events.title: Police clear the camp
events.body: After a Delhi Police leadership change, officers remove barricades and forcibly move Wangchuk to a hospital.

events.title: March on Parliament
events.body: Over 10,000 march toward Parliament; police respond with tear gas and batons. Around 180 reported injured, 70 detained.

events.title: Marches paused, camp rebuilt
events.body: Leaders pause street marches for safety while continuing the protest; demonstrators rebuild the demolished camp.
